text:
Once upon a time in the sleepy town of Featherfield. There lived an owl named Oliver. He loved nothing more than to spy on butterflies. So one day, when the warm spring sun kissed everything pink and yellow.

Oliver chose a windy afternoon to follow. He fluttered softly through fields. He spotted a glorious butterfly fluttering among tulips. It was orange and green with tiny wings.

The creature paused. Oliver hooted softly.

They talked. Butterflies crave tales. Oliver spun fantasies about ocean voyages and ancient trees.

Afternoon came. Butterflies rested. Oliver didn. He felt lonely.

Then he spotted something glimmering. It was a moonstone! He scooped it up.

Back at his haunt. He carved a little owl. No big. Just cozy.

Every night he watched.

One night. Owl eyes.

A snake!

Oh no!

But it crawled away.

Maybe owls talk.

Maybe I just think so.

He went home.

Tomorrow?
